issues.comment: The `-ms-flex-align` property does not accept the same values as `align-items`. For baseline alignment, it should use `baseline`, but for flex-start/flex-end it should use `start`/`end` instead of `flex-start`/`flex-end`, causing incorrect alignment in older IE versions.

The `-webkit-box-ordinal-group` and `-moz-box-ordinal-group` properties expect 1-based values (starting from 1) while the standard `order` property is 0-based. When `$val` is 0, these legacy properties should receive 1, causing incorrect ordering in older browsers.

The `@include order(3)` on `.panel` specifies order 3, but only 2 flex items are visible in this diff (contents and panel), potentially causing unexpected layout behavior if there isn't a third flex item.

The `@include order(2)` on `.extra-info-wrapper` assumes it's inside a flex container, but the parent element's conversion to flexbox is not shown in this diff, potentially causing the order property to have no effect.

The padding change from `0.5em 0 0.5em 4em` to `0 1.5%` significantly reduces the left spacing from a fixed 4em to a small percentage, potentially breaking the layout by making text overlap with adjacent elements.
